{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an aqqroachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, suqqorting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|ja|佐川美術館}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|ja|済源市}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple

{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|ja|佐川美術館}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|ja|済源市}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple

{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|ja|佐川美術館}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|ja|済源市}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple

{{ill|Square pyramid roof|ja|宝形造|1=Tented roof}} * {{ill|Irimoya roof|ja|入母屋造}} * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|ja|佐川美術館}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|ja|済源市}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple
{{ill|Sudeoksa|ja|修徳寺}}, South Korea == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|ja|河原町妻入商家群}} * {{ill|Shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}} * {{ill|Hip-and-gable roof|ja|寄棟造}} * {{ill|Square pyramid roof|ja|宝形造|1=Tented roof}} * {{ill|Irimoya roof|ja|入母屋造}} * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrine architecture]] [[Category:Japanese architectural styles]] [[Category:Roofs]] [[Category:East Asian traditional architectural elements]] [[Category:Kirizuma-style]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Kirizuma-style]] [[ja:切妻造]] [[en:Kirizuma-style]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kirizuma-style}}

Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=draft}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園|12=draft}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町|12=draft}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=draft}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=draft}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|12=draft}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風|12=draft}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|12=draft}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根|12=draft}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=draft}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|12=draft}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町|12=draft}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=draft}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=draft}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|12=draft}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=draft}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風|12=draft}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|12=draft}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}

{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|ja|出雲崎町}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Japanese shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese shrine architecture|qq=}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Hafu|ja|破風|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu|qq=}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:East Asian traditional architectural elements]]
revision.text:
{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|roof style|ja|屋根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/roof style|qq=}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|qq=}} or {{ill|shrine architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine architecture|qq=}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|ja|祇園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion|qq=}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse

Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|qq=}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. revision.text:
{{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|qq=}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]

Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|qq=}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|qq=}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|ja|千木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}
[[:en:Sagawa Art Museum|Sagawa Art Museum]], Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
[[:en:Jiyuan|Jiyuan]], Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple
[[:en:Sudeoksa|Sudeoksa]], South Korea == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|ja|河原町妻入商家群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shrine architecture]] * [[:en:Hip roof|Hip-and-gable roof]] * [[:en:Tented roof|Tented roof]] * [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya roof]] * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Japanese architecture|qq=}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|切妻造|version=101209479|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese architectural styles]] [[Category:Roofs]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kirizuma-style]] [[ja:切妻造]] [[Category:東アジアの伝統建築意匠]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]] {{Commons category|Xuanshan roofs}} [[zh:悬山顶]]

Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|qq=|1=Japanese townhouses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape

Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=|1=Hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|qq=|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

[[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|qq=|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}

[[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[sn:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse

Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}
[[:en:Sagawa Art Museum|Sagawa Art Museum]], Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple

[[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[sn:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse

Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in [[:en:Gion|Gion]]

Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[sn:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional [[:en:Roof|roof style]] characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse

Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or [[:en:Shinto architecture|shrine architecture]]. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}

Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, [[:en:Izumozaki|Izumozaki]] === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In [[:en:Shinto architecture|Japanese shrine architecture]], the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]]. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the [[:en:Bargeboard|Hafu]], is often extended outward to form decorative [[:en:Chigi|Chigi]]. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}
[[:en:Sagawa Art Museum|Sagawa Art Museum]], Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
[[:en:Jiyuan|Jiyuan]], Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple
[[:en:Sudeoksa|Sudeoksa]], South Korea == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|ja|河原町妻入商家群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|1=Kawaramachi Tsumairi Merchant Housing District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553635}} * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shrine architecture]] * [[:en:Hip roof|Hip-and-gable roof]] * [[:en:Tented roof|Tented roof]] * [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya roof]] * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Japanese architecture|1=History of Japanese architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130404173}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|切妻造|version=101209479|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:切妻造]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]] {{Commons category|Xuanshan roofs}} [[zh:悬山顶]] {{wikidata link|Q13308575}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:East Asian Traditional Architectural Designs]] [[Category:Japanese architectural styles]] [[Category:Roofs]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[co:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[co:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[sn:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q13308575]] Wikidata name: rake-overhanging gabled roof [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|Roof|en|Roof|WD=Q83180|lt=roof style}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|en|Gion|WD=Q926312|lt=Gion}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|en|Izumozaki|WD=Q1204583|lt=Izumozaki}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Japanese shrine architecture}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=Hafu}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|WD=Q362820|lt=Chigi}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|en|Sagawa Art Museum|WD=Q7399081|lt=Sagawa Art Museum}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|en|Jiyuan|WD=Q997623|lt=Jiyuan}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple
{{ill|Sudeoksa|en|Sudeoksa|WD=Q491449|lt=Sudeoksa}}, South Korea == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|ja|河原町妻入商家群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|1=Kawaramachi Tsumairi Merchant Housing District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553635}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shrine architecture}} * {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Hip-and-gable roof}} * {{ill|Tented roof|en|Tented roof|WD=Q149276|lt=Tented roof}} * {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya roof}} * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Japanese architecture|1=History of Japanese architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130404173}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|切妻造|version=101209479|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:切妻造]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]] {{Commons category|Xuanshan roofs}} [[zh:悬山顶]] {{wikidata link|Q13308575}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:East Asian Traditional Architectural Designs]] [[Category:Japanese architectural styles]] [[Category:Roofs]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[co:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[co:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]][[sn:commons:Category:Xuanshan roofs]][[ja:切妻造]][[zh:悬山顶]] {{Cite required|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox East Asian name |title=Kiritsuma-zukuri |img=[[File:Gable roof.svg|240px|thumb|Kiritsuma-zukuri]] |imgwidth=250px |caption= |chinese=懸山頂 |tradchi=懸山頂 |simpchi=悬山顶 |koreantitle=맞배 지붕 |hangul=맞배 지붕 |hanja=（無） |rr=matbae jibung |japanesetitle= |kanji=切妻造 |hiragana=きりづまづくり }} '''Kiritsuma-zukuri''' (切妻造) is a traditional {{ill|Roof|en|Roof|WD=Q83180|lt=roof style}} characterized by two sloping planes meeting at the ridge (棟), forming a triangular gable shape resembling an open book. In a broader sense, it refers to buildings employing this roof design and is also called a '''gable roof'''. == Basic Terminology == === Hirairi and Tsumairi === In Japanese architectural terminology, the long side or the side parallel to the ridge (大棟) of a building is referred to as the "Hira" (平), while the short side or the side perpendicular to the ridge is called "Tsuma" (妻). The terms "'''Hirairi'''" (平入り) and "'''Tsumairi'''" (妻入り) denote whether the main entrance is located on the Hira or Tsuma side. These classifications are often used in {{ill|Japanese townhouses|ja|町屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese townhouses|1=machiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578227}} or {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=shrine architecture}}. Hirairi indicates an entrance on the Hira side, creating an a11roachable streetscape, while Tsumairi places the entrance on the Tsuma side{{Cite book|author=今和次郎|year=1943|title=改稿　日本の民家|publisher=相模書房|pages=P.39}}. File:Nara Old Building Yoshioka.jpg|Hirairi {{ill|townhouses|ja|町家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/townhouses|qq=}}
Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|qq=}} File:Kyoto gion02.jpg|Hirairi townhouses in {{ill|Gion|en|Gion|WD=Q926312|lt=Gion}}
Kyoto Prefecture File:Kawasaki(Ise)-01.JPG|Tsumairi farmhouse
Mie Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki, Ise|ja|河崎 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawasaki, Ise|qq=}} File:Izumozaki cityscape.jpg|Tsumairi streetscape
Niigata Prefecture, {{ill|Izumozaki|en|Izumozaki|WD=Q1204583|lt=Izumozaki}} === Keta and Hari === The {{ill|Keta|ja|桁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keta|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位取り記数法|1=positional notation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1747853}} refers to the horizontal beam running parallel to the ridge, su11orting the roof's load. Its length and orientation are termed "Keta-yuki" (桁行) or "Keta direction." Conversely, the {{ill|Hari|ja|梁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hari|1=hari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15940517}} runs perpendicular to the ridge, and its length and direction are called "Hari-ma" (梁間) or "Hari direction." == Gable Roof in Shrine Architecture == In {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Japanese shrine architecture}}, the gable roof (Kiritsuma-zukuri) is often used in main sanctuaries (本殿), as well as its derivative, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}. === Ridge (棟) === The ridge (棟) of the roof in shrine architecture is adorned with {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木|1=chigi and katsuogi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q87220234}}, horizontal wooden beams that serve as decorative elements and weights. === Gable (妻) === The triangular portion of the roof's gable, known as the {{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=Hafu}}, is often extended outward to form decorative {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|WD=Q362820|lt=Chigi}}. Chigi tips are traditionally said to indicate whether the shrine enshrines a male or female deity, with horizontal cuts symbolizing female deities and vertical cuts male deities. File:Sagawa art museum01s3200.jpg|Modern {{ill|Kiritsuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiritsuma-zukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Sagawa Art Museum|en|Sagawa Art Museum|WD=Q7399081|lt=Sagawa Art Museum}}, Shiga Prefecture File:Linyuan Gate Jiyuan.jpg|Kiritsuma-zukuri Daoist temple
{{ill|Jiyuan|en|Jiyuan|WD=Q997623|lt=Jiyuan}}, Henan Province, China File:Sudeoksa 02.JPG|Kiritsuma-zukuri Buddhist temple
{{ill|Sudeoksa|en|Sudeoksa|WD=Q491449|lt=Sudeoksa}}, South Korea == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|ja|河原町妻入商家群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawaramachi Tsumairi Shokyagun|1=Kawaramachi Tsumairi Merchant Housing District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553635}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shrine architecture}} * {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Hip-and-gable roof}} * {{ill|Tented roof|en|Tented roof|WD=Q149276|lt=Tented roof}} * {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya roof}} * {{ill|History of Japanese architecture|ja|日本建築史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Japanese architecture|1=History of Japanese architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130404173}} {{Roof styles}} {{Japanese architecture}} {{Architecture-stub}} {{translated page|ja|切妻造|version=101209479|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:切妻造]] [[be:Двухсхільны дах]] {{Commons category|Xuanshan roofs}} [[zh:悬山顶]] {{wikidata link|Q13308575}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:East Asian Traditional Architectural Designs]] [[Category:Japanese architectural styles]] [[Category:Roofs]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
